jokes.text: If you don't like 'em, this is called a door, you can walk right through it.
jokes.setup: Deangelo announcing big changes coming
jokes.punchline: Patronizingly explains what a door is
jokes.explanation: Solid character-driven condescension with good economy, but fairly predictable tough-boss humor

jokes.text: Full ride, deal with it. Seriously? Stone-cold seriously.
jokes.setup: Deangelo announcing Darryl gets business school
jokes.punchline: Treating good news like a threat with 'deal with it' attitude
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Deangelo's aggressive delivery of positive news creates absurd contrast

jokes.text: They are trying to figure me out, and I don't like it. Once they figure me out, they start to tell me what I want to hear.
jokes.setup: Deangelo in talking head explaining his management philosophy
jokes.punchline: Backwards logic about not wanting employees to please him
jokes.explanation: Strong character insight - reveals Deangelo's paranoid, contradictory management style

jokes.text: Don't thank me. Hey, don't thank me, guy.
jokes.setup: Toby thanks Deangelo for new chair
jokes.punchline: Deangelo aggressively refuses thanks multiple times
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Deangelo's need to stay emotionally distant

jokes.text: Here's a bowl of ice cream, you either like it or you don't. That's my attitude right now in this room, that's my attitude on 'Ice Cream Thursdays.'
jokes.setup: Deangelo explaining his don't-care attitude
jokes.punchline: Bizarre ice cream metaphor extending to actual ice cream policy
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist moment - the metaphor becoming literal policy is perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: But I could see how some people might think that they're bad.
jokes.setup: Kevin saying changes sound great
jokes.punchline: Immediately hedging with obvious contradiction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin moment - his attempt to play both sides reveals his simple logic

jokes.text: That is a... An astute observation, Kevin. Kev's got me pegged.
jokes.setup: Kevin making obvious non-observation
jokes.punchline: Deangelo genuinely impressed by Kevin's fence-sitting
jokes.explanation: Great character pairing - Deangelo's paranoia makes Kevin's obvious statement seem insightful

jokes.text: I found your new executive assistant, my friend, Carla. She's got great experience, we even considered making her Cece's godmother, but she had this boyfriend at the time.
jokes.setup: Pam recommending her friend for job
jokes.punchline: Bizarre personal detail about godmother consideration
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Pam's oversharing, but setup is too long for payoff

jokes.text: Oh, there is an inner circle. Oh, yeah. There is no inner circle.
jokes.setup: Discussion about whether inner circle exists
jokes.punchline: Direct contradiction in consecutive lines
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and structure in the rapid contradiction, though concept is simple

jokes.text: I also say that because I am also in the inner circle.
jokes.setup: Andy claiming there's no inner circle
jokes.punchline: Immediately admitting he's in it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - his need to boast undermines his own argument

jokes.text: Did you get that, ma? Your boy, Kevin Malone, is in the inner circle, which doesn't exist.
jokes.setup: Kevin bragging to camera about inner circle
jokes.punchline: Contradicts himself in same sentence
jokes.explanation: Excellent Kevin moment - 'Did you get that, ma?' shows his pride while the contradiction reveals his confusion

jokes.text: It's just that I own the coffee shop. So once you've seen sausage being made, all you wanna do is make sausage 'cause it's so much fun.
jokes.setup: Deangelo declining coffee, referencing sausage-making metaphor
jokes.punchline: Completely misuses the 'sausage being made' idiom
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of common idiom - usually about not wanting to see process, he wants to make more

jokes.text: No matter how many times I reach out to Dwight, he doesn't seem to want anything to do with me. It reminds me of my relationship with my son. Except there, I'm the Dwight.
jokes.setup: Deangelo comparing relationship with Dwight to his son
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's the one being rejected by his son
jokes.explanation: Dark and revealing character moment with excellent structure - the realization lands perfectly

jokes.text: Or my other idea, 50,000 tiny clients.
jokes.setup: Discussion about going after biggest client
jokes.punchline: Absurd alternative presented matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and absurdist logic - the mathematical opposite presented as equivalent strategy

jokes.text: That's Toys 'R' Us, I think. No, that's definitely a knockoff. You can feel the center of gravity is off.
jokes.setup: Examining basketball quality
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fake expertise about toy basketball physics
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Deangelo's need to appear knowledgeable about everything

jokes.text: So, to make things simpler for Deangelo, I just, without lying, strongly implied that I'm Kelly's supervisor.
jokes.setup: Ryan explaining his strategy with Deangelo
jokes.punchline: 'Without lying' while describing deception
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan - the semantic hair-splitting while admitting manipulation is pure character

jokes.text: Yeah, but you lie all the time. You lie for no reason. Ryan, you just like to lie. I'd die for you, too.
jokes.setup: Kelly calling out Ryan's lying
jokes.punchline: Ryan's romantic escalation to dying for her
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic - Ryan's dramatic deflection from being caught lying

jokes.text: You know, I have a cousin who cracked the secret formula for a certain popular cola that I shall not name. So I've never had to buy it. True story. I just drink my cousin's.
jokes.setup: Deangelo making casual conversation
jokes.punchline: Elaborate lie about cola formula and homemade cola
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Deangelo's compulsive lying and need to seem connected

jokes.text: Congratulations on your one cousin. I have 70, each one better than the last.
jokes.setup: Deangelo bragging about cousin
jokes.punchline: Dwight's absurd numerical escalation and ranking system
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - the precise number and ranking system for cousins is beautifully absurd

jokes.text: Yes! No! Yes! Yes! No! No! Yes, yes, yes, yes, yes! Yes, yes, yes, yes, yes... Uh-oh.
jokes.setup: Deangelo saying he'll win Dwight over
jokes.punchline: Escalating yes/no battle ending in 'Uh-oh'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure, though the 'uh-oh' suggests visual payoff we can't see

jokes.text: This former administrative assistant misspelled 'administrative,' and 'assistant.'
jokes.setup: Reviewing resumes for assistant position
jokes.punchline: Can't spell the job title they're applying for
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor with good economy, but fairly predictable

jokes.text: Under special skills, Mr. Don Finer put juggling. What's wrong with juggling, Darryl? I'm a big juggler. I actually perform a motivational juggling routine.
jokes.setup: Reading resume with juggling as skill
jokes.punchline: Deangelo defensively reveals he also juggles motivationally
jokes.explanation: Good setup for extended sequence - 'motivational juggling routine' is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: What would you say this room is? 300 square feet? 320? Three-twenty, just free-balling it. It's a little cramped. How many square feet out there? Seventeen, eighteen hundo?
jokes.setup: Deangelo explaining why he can't juggle in the room
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fake expertise about office square footage
jokes.explanation: Shows Deangelo's compulsive expertise claims, though setup is lengthy for modest payoff

jokes.text: Sorry, I never touch another juggler's instruments.
jokes.setup: Andy offering juggling balls
jokes.punchline: Treating juggling with sacred performer etiquette
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment - treating office juggling with professional performer reverence

jokes.text: Why don't I just do my routine without the juggling balls? Prepare to go into the danger zone.
jokes.setup: Deangelo offering to juggle without balls
jokes.punchline: Treating invisible juggling as dangerous
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup for extended sequence - the danger zone reference for mime juggling is perfect

jokes.text: Phyllis, where's Phyllis? Here. Do you believe in me, Phyllis? Yes. 'Cause I believe in you. Feel that connection?
jokes.setup: Deangelo mid-fake juggling routine
jokes.punchline: Forced inspirational moment with confused Phyllis
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - the forced intimacy during fake performance lands well

jokes.text: He didn't drop a single ball.
jokes.setup: Deangelo finished fake juggling routine
jokes.punchline: Kevin's literal observation about invisible performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin line - his literal interpretation of the fake performance is brilliantly obtuse

jokes.text: What could he possibly stand to gain from a fake juggling routine? What could he possibly stand to gain from a real juggling routine?
jokes.setup: Dwight questioning Deangelo's fake juggling
jokes.punchline: Realizing real juggling is equally pointless
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structure - Dwight's logical mind leads him to the deeper absurdity

jokes.text: I wrote my own companion piece to The Vagina Monologues, called The Penis Apologies. So I know a thing or two.
jokes.setup: Andy claiming he'd recognize sexism
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fake feminist credentials with terrible title
jokes.explanation: Excellent Andy moment - 'The Penis Apologies' is perfectly wrong-headed male feminism

jokes.text: And if he doesn't listen, then he can kiss his penis goodbye. Snip-snip. Am I right, girls?
jokes.setup: Angela suggesting Jim talk to Deangelo about sexism
jokes.punchline: Casual castration threat with 'girls' appeal
jokes.explanation: Good dark Angela moment - the casual violence paired with 'am I right, girls' lands well

jokes.text: Oh, like, nobody, it was... Pam? Was it Pam? Oh, my gosh. That sounds like Pam. You know how she gets. Kevin, she can get really bitchy.
jokes.setup: Deangelo asking who thinks he's sexist
jokes.punchline: Kevin immediately throwing Pam under bus with 'bitchy'
jokes.explanation: Good cringe escalation as situation spins out of Jim's control

jokes.text: Raise your hand if you have a vagina. Raise your hand if someone you love has a vagina.
jokes.setup: Deangelo trying to prove he's not sexist
jokes.punchline: Incredibly inappropriate office survey about genitalia
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy - Deangelo's attempt to prove inclusivity is spectacularly inappropriate

jokes.text: What about Deangelo's hand? Oh, wow. He's got 'em both up.
jokes.setup: Deangelo raising hands for both vagina questions
jokes.punchline: Someone noting he qualifies for both categories
jokes.explanation: Decent observational moment extending the cringe sequence

jokes.text: NBA, WNBA. One is a sport, one is a joke. I love sports, I love jokes. Room for all.
jokes.setup: Dwight asked about gender equality
jokes.punchline: Casually dismisses women's sports but claims to love both
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - the brutal honesty followed by false diplomacy is expertly crafted

jokes.text: A law office? No, Anthropologie. 'We don't have this and that size.' Pretty lame.
jokes.setup: Jordan explaining previous job
jokes.punchline: Dismisses dream retail job as lame with bad impression
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Jordan's cluelessness about Kelly's interests

jokes.text: Yeah. Yup. Wow. No corporate experience whatsoever. I didn't want anyone with any bad habits.
jokes.setup: Pam questioning Jordan's lack of experience
jokes.punchline: Deangelo spinning inexperience as advantage
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Deangelo's backwards hiring logic

jokes.text: What's up, man cave?
jokes.setup: Andy trying to join inner circle meeting
jokes.punchline: Awkward greeting trying to sound cool
jokes.explanation: Decent Andy moment but fairly predictable try-hard behavior

jokes.text: Jim watching through window, clearly wanting in but unable to enter
jokes.setup: Jim excluded from inner circle meeting
jokes.punchline: Visual of him awkwardly standing outside
jokes.explanation: Good visual cringe moment - Jim's exclusion creates uncomfortable sympathy

jokes.text: Tell your whore to leave me alone!
jokes.setup: Jordan asking if Dwight needs anything
jokes.punchline: Dwight's brutal rejection via Jordan as messenger
jokes.explanation: Strong Dwight moment - the harshness delivered through intermediary is perfectly awkward

jokes.text: So this is my life until I win the lottery or Pam finally writes that series of young adult books. So, one afternoon while walking home from school, quirky 10th-grader Becky Walters finds a wounded Pegasus in the woods. And she becomes 'The Horse Flyer.'
jokes.setup: Jim lamenting his situation
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fantasy about Pam's nonexistent book series
jokes.explanation: Great Jim moment - the specific YA fantasy details show his creative desperation

jokes.text: No, he's just a big fraud, Deangelo. He's like Rango. He doesn't work here, basically, just like the way Rango didn't save those animals. It was just a big misunderstanding.
jokes.setup: Kelly trying to expose Ryan
jokes.punchline: Convoluted Rango movie comparison that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Decent Kelly moment showing her confused pop culture analogies, but too convoluted

jokes.text: I did not see Rango.
jokes.setup: Kelly's elaborate Rango comparison
jokes.punchline: Ryan's flat denial of seeing the movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan response - completely deflates Kelly's comparison with simple fact

jokes.text: Total brain burp. I'm no MJ. Please. I can do his dunk from free-throw line, though.
jokes.setup: Deangelo mistakenly called Magic Jordan instead of Michael Jordan
jokes.punchline: Immediately claims he can do Michael Jordan's signature move
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Deangelo's false modesty immediately undercut by boasting

jokes.text: Mad respect from a brother! The man is paying me to take Chinese. I will say what I need to say. And soon, I will say it in Chinese.
jokes.setup: Darryl praising Deangelo's basketball skills
jokes.punchline: Admits he's only being supportive because of business school benefit
jokes.explanation: Great Darryl moment - honest about his motivations with good callback structure

jokes.text: Only because no one has called NASA to request a lift off.
jokes.setup: Pressured to actually do the free-throw line dunk
jokes.punchline: Absurd excuse about needing NASA assistance
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist excuse - NASA requirement for basketball dunk is perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: If I wanted to see a pissing contest, I'd lock Mose in the chicken coop.
jokes.setup: Dwight commenting on basketball confrontation
jokes.punchline: Bizarre rural comparison involving his cousin and chickens
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight line - the specific rural imagery and casual family cruelty is perfect

jokes.text: Damn it, Dwight, enough! Get your ass downstairs, or find a new place to sell paper!
jokes.setup: Dwight's dismissive comment about pissing contest
jokes.punchline: Deangelo's explosive overreaction and job threat
jokes.explanation: Good escalation moment showing Deangelo's thin skin and poor management

jokes.text: Okay, a little about me. I respond to strong leadership.
jokes.setup: Dwight immediately complying after being yelled at
jokes.punchline: Explaining his submission as attraction to leadership
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment - rationalizes being bullied as admiration for authority

jokes.text: Deangelo falls and hits his head attempting the dunk, lying motionless on basketball court
jokes.setup: Deangelo finally attempts the impossible dunk he's been claiming he can do
jokes.punchline: Complete failure resulting in serious injury
jokes.explanation: Strong physical comedy payoff - all his boasting leads to spectacular failure

jokes.text: Who should I say is calling? Erin.
jokes.setup: Erin told to call 911 for emergency
jokes.punchline: Asks who she should say is calling 911
jokes.explanation: Perfect Erin moment - her sweet confusion about emergency protocol is endearing and funny
